message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

I'd like your help with an itch, while we wait some other chats: BCG used to say that the bash filename 'word' following the prefix ought to be uppercase, but sometimes I used lowercase names, and now the codebase is heterogeneous.  Would you form an opinion on what is clearer?  I wonder that we ought to consolidate before MVP on one pattern for professional appearance.  It is a lower risk change I think.  Don't change code mind you, just form an opinion.

addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Happy to dig into this. Let me survey the actual distribution of the two patterns across the kits before forming a view.

input.description: Find filename naming guidance in BCG

content.content:
348-
349-# Tinder constants (pure string literals or tinder-on-tinder composition — available at source time)
350:# «PREFIX»_some_path="fixed-filename.env"
351-# «PREFIX»_some_prefix="prefix_"
352-
353-######################################################################
354-# Internal Functions (z«prefix»_*)
355-
356-z«prefix»_kindle() {
357-  test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module «prefix» already kindled"
358-
--
374-  Z«PREFIX»_TEMP_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_temp.txt"  # Non-secret data only
375-  Z«PREFIX»_RESULT_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_«command»_«step_number»_result.json"
376:  Z«PREFIX»_CONFIG_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_«command»_«step_number»_config_"     # rest of file name appended on use
377:  Z«PREFIX»_MANIFEST_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_«command»_«step_number»_manifest_"   # rest of file name appended on use
378-
379-  # Temporary files (if using BURD_OUTPUT_DIR)
380-  Z«PREFIX»_OUTPUT_RESULT="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/result.txt"
381-
382-  Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED=1
383-}
384-
385-z«prefix»_sentinel() {
--
603-```
604-
605:**Stderr file naming**: Use a kindle `_PREFIX` constant (grouped with other temp file prefixes for collision visibility) and append a discriminator at the usage site.
606-
607-**In loops**, use an auto-incrementing integer for uniqueness rather than trusting loop data values to be unique:
608-
609-```bash
610-# In kindle — grouped with sibling temp file prefixes
611-Z«PREFIX»_«COMMAND»_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_«command»_"
612-
613-# In command function — integer discriminator ensures uniqueness
--
859-- Enroll validates inputs and appends atomically to all rolls
860-- Recite functions (see below) provide read-only access after kindle completes
861:- Variable naming: `z_«prefix»_«name»_roll` for all roll arrays (internal state, initialized in kindle)
862-
863-**Example** (using synthetic notation — «reg» owns the rolls, «con» consumes them):
864-
865-```bash
866-# Module «reg» — owns the rolls
867-z«reg»_kindle() {
868-  test -z "${Z«REG»_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "already kindled"
869-
--
1056-- **Neither**: Use temp files for multi-step pipelines, direct `|| buc_die` for simple failures
1057-
1058:## Naming Convention Patterns
1059-
1060-### ✅ Correct Patterns
1061-
1062-| Element                      | Pattern                      | Example                      | Location | Name/ Case Constraints        |
1063-|------------------------------|------------------------------|------------------------------|----------|-------------------------------|
1064-| Module prefix                | `[a-z]{2,4}_`                | `rbv_`, `buc_`, `auth_`      | Both     | single char group             |
1065-| Implementation file          | `«prefix»_«name».sh`         | `rbv_podman.sh`              | N/A      | lowercase (prefer 1 word, allow snake_case) |
1066-| CLI file                     | `«prefix»_cli.sh`            | `rbv_cli.sh`                 | N/A      | snake_case (fixed)            |
1067-| Decomposition CLI            | `«prefix»0_cli.sh`           | —                            | N/A      | fixed (decomposed module)     |
1068:| Decomposition entry          | `«prefix»0_«ModuleName».sh`  | —                            | N/A      | PascalCase «ModuleName»        |
1069:| Decomposition cluster        | `«prefix»«g»_«Name».sh`      | —                            | N/A      | gesture letter + PascalCase «Name» |
1070-| Public functions             | `«prefix»_«command»`         | `rbv_init`, `rbv_start`      | Impl     | snake_case (usually one word) |
1071-| Internal functions           | `z«prefix»_«name»`           | `zrbv_validate_pat`          | Impl     | snake_case (often multi word) |
1072-| Furnish function             | `z«prefix»_furnish`          | `zrbv_furnish`               | CLI      | fixed name                    |
1073-| Kindle function              | `z«prefix»_kindle`           | `zrbv_kindle`                | Impl     | fixed name                    |
1074-| Sentinel function            | `z«prefix»_sentinel`         | `zrbv_sentinel`              | Impl     | fixed name                    |
1075-| Predicate Internal functions | `z«prefix»_«name»_predicate` | `zrbv_file_exists_predicate` | Impl     | snake_case                    |
1076-| Capture Internal functions   | `z«prefix»_«name»_capture`   | `zrbv_get_token_capture`     | Impl     | snake_case                    |
1077-| Capture Public functions     | `«prefix»_«name»_capture`    | `rbv_get_token_capture`      | Impl     | snake_case                    |
--
1085-| Enroll return vars           | `z_«funcname»_«retval»`      | `z_«prefix»_enroll_name`     | Impl     | snake_case (func name verbatim)|
1086-| Testbench file               | `«prefix»tb_testbench.sh`    | `rbtb_testbench.sh`          | N/A      | fixed name                    |
1087:| Test case file               | `«prefix»tc«xx»_«Name».sh`  | `rbtckk_KickTires.sh`        | N/A      | PascalCase name               |
1088-| Fixture baste function       | `z«tb»_«name»_baste`        | `zrbtb_ark_baste`            | Testbench| snake_case, fixture layer     |
1089-| Case function                | `«tc»_«name»_tcase`         | `rbtckk_false_tcase`         | Test case| snake_case, case layer        |
1090-| Yawp function (public)       | `«prefix»_«name»_yawp`      | `buyy_cmd_yawp`              | Impl     | snake_case                    |
1091-| Yawp function (internal)     | `z«prefix»_«name»_yawp`     | `zbuyf_resolve_yawp`         | Impl     | snake_case                    |
1092-| Yawp return variable         | `z_«module»_«group»`        | `z_buym_yelp`                | Impl     | snake_case, mutable kindle state |

lastPrompt: I'd like your help with an itch, while we wait some other chats: BCG used to say that the bash filename 'word' following the prefix ought to be uppercase, but sometimes I used lowercase names, and now…

content.text:
I've got what I need — current BCG (the Naming Convention table around line 1058) actually already picked a side for plain modules: implementation file = `«prefix»_«name».sh` **lowercase**, with PascalCase carved out for decomposition entries/clusters (`rbfc0_FoundryCore.sh`, `rbfca_StepAssembly.sh`) and test-case files. The CLAUDE.md mint table still shows the old `prefix_Word.ext` → `rbga_ArtifactRegistry.sh` form, so the two documents disagree with each other as well as with the tree.

## What the tree actually looks like

- **rbk**: ~41 PascalCase content files (`rba_Auth.sh`, `rbga_ArtifactRegistry.sh`, all the decomposed `rbfc*`/`rbfl*`/`rbld*` clusters) against a uniform lowercase wall of `*_cli.sh` / `*_regime.sh` partners — plus a few lowercase content strays (`rbfk_kludge.sh`, `rbob_bottle.sh`, `rboo_observe.sh`, `rbcr_render.sh`, `rbndb_base.sh`).
- **buk, jjk, cmk, vvk, vok, apck**: all-lowercase, with exactly one PascalCase stray (`bupr_PresentationRegime.sh`).

So the heterogeneity is really "rbk's older and decomposed modules kept the old uppercase rule; everything else follows the new lowercase rule."

## My opinion: consolidate to all-lowercase, one rule

`«prefix»_«snake_name».sh`, prefer one word — and retire the PascalCase carve-outs for decomposition clusters and test cases too. Reasons, strongest first:

1. **Register consistency.** Every other bash identifier class is already case-uniform with a first-segment-prefix rule: functions `buc_log_args`, variables `BURC_PROJECT_ROOT`. Filenames as `prefix_Word` are a third register that matches neither. The supposed ambiguity PascalCase resolves (where does the prefix end?) isn't real — the prefix is always the first underscore-delimited segment, the same parse readers already do on every function name.

2. **Blast radius favors this direction.** BUK/JJK/VVK are *distributed* kits — renaming `buc_command.sh` to `buc_Command.sh` would ripple into every consumer install. The uppercase population is almost entirely rbk-internal, where renames are cheap and pre-MVP is exactly the time. Consolidating uppercase would mean ~45 renames on the shipped surface; consolidating lowercase means ~42 renames in your own house.

3. **It's where the law already drifted.** BCG's current table says lowercase for plain impl files; the majority of files (everything outside rbk plus rbk's cli/regime families) comply. You'd be finishing a migration, not starting one.

4. **Professional appearance**, since that's your framing: all-lowercase snake_case is the overwhelming convention for shell in the wild; mixed-case `.sh` filenames read as house-idiosyncratic to an outside eye.

On the decomposition-cluster carve-out specifically: I don't think the case there is load-bearing. The `0`-entry and gesture-letter structure (`rbfc0_`, `rbfca_`) already carries the "decomposed family" signal; `rbfca_step_assembly.sh` loses nothing.

What I'd consciously give up: in `ls Tools/rbk/`, the uppercase files currently pop as "where the logic lives" against the `_cli.sh`/`_regime.sh` boilerplate wall. That's a real but minor reading aid, currently unreliable anyway because of the strays — and the role-suffix words themselves still carry it.

**The alternative**, one line: codify the hybrid crisply (lowercase = fixed structural roles, PascalCase = content) and just fix the ~7 strays — smallest possible diff, but it leaves buk's content files (`buc_command.sh`, `but_test.sh`) on the "wrong" side or forces distributed-kit renames, which is why I don't favor it.

Untouched universes either way: doc files (`RBS0-SpecTop.adoc`) and tabtarget frontispieces (`rbw-cC.Charge.tadmor.sh`) are different grammars with different delimiters and should keep their casing.

Mechanical footnotes if this becomes a heat: many renames are case-only (`rba_Auth.sh` → `rba_auth.sh`), which on macOS's case-insensitive filesystem want `git mv` and a little care around branch crossings; and the sweep touches sourcing lines, the zipper registry, the rbk acronym map, the CLAUDE.md mint table row, and BCG's three PascalCase table rows. All grep-able, all low-risk — I agree with your read that this is a cheap consolidation relative to the polish it buys.

message.content: Remind me the upper case files you were thinking to give up on?

content.text:
These are the PascalCase files that would get renamed down to lowercase — all in rbk except one buk stray:

**Plain modules (pre-dating the lowercase rule):**
- `rba_Auth.sh`
- `rbcc_Constants.sh`
- `rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh`
- `rbga_ArtifactRegistry.sh`
- `rbgb_Buckets.sh`
- `rbgc_Constants.sh`
- `rbgd_DepotConstants.sh`
- `rbge_Rest.sh`
- `rbgg_Governor.sh`
- `rbgi_IAM.sh`
- `rbgl_GarLayout.sh`
- `rbgo_OAuth.sh`
- `rbgp_Payor.sh`
- `rbgv_AccessProbe.sh`
- `rbq_Qualify.sh`
- `rbuh_Http.sh`
- `rbupmis_Scrub.sh`

**Single-file foundry modules with cli partners:**
- `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh`
- `rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh`
- `rbfr_FoundryRetriever.sh`
- `rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh`

**Decomposed families (currently PascalCase by BCG's carve-out, which I'm proposing to retire):**
- `rbfc0_FoundryCore.sh`, `rbfca_StepAssembly.sh`, `rbfcb_BuildHost.sh`, `rbfcg_GarRest.sh`, `rbfcp_Plumb.sh`, `rbfcv_VesselResolution.sh`
- `rbfl0_FoundryLedger.sh`, `rbfld_Delete.sh`, `rbfli_Inscribe.sh`, `rbfln_Inventory.sh`, `rbflw_Wrest.sh`, `rbfly_Yoke.sh`
- `rbld0_Lode.sh`, `rbldb_Bole.sh`, `rbldd_Delete.sh`, `rbldl_Lifecycle.sh`, `rbldr_Reliquary.sh`, `rblds_Spine.sh`, `rbldv_Immure.sh`, `rbldw_Underpin.sh`

**The one buk stray:**
- `bupr_PresentationRegime.sh`

That's 42 files. Plus, if the test-case-file carve-out (`«prefix»tc«xx»_«Name».sh`, e.g. `rbtckk_KickTires.sh`) goes too, whatever test case files exist under that pattern — I didn't enumerate those.

A note while listing: the multi-word ones are where the rename has bite — `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` becomes `rbfd_foundry_director_build.sh`, which is noticeably longer-feeling. If any of those names feel heavy in snake_case, the BCG "prefer 1 word" guidance is the moment to shorten the word part itself (e.g. the prefix already says foundry-director — the word part could slim down), rather than to keep the case.

content: You asked my opinion on consolidating bash filename casing before MVP; I recommend all-lowercase snake_case and listed the 42 PascalCase files (mostly rbk) that would rename. Next action: your call on whether to slate it as a heat.

message.content:
That looks decent, and I'm actually for shortening these to single word where you suggest.  I seem to have misunderstood though from your words pasted below: are you pitching leaving some uppercase?  Did you consider all of BUK for snake casing?  I think I like this plan.



  What I'd consciously give up: in ls Tools/rbk/, the uppercase files currently pop as "where the logic lives"
  against the _cli.sh/_regime.sh boilerplate wall. That's a real but minor reading aid, currently unreliable
  anyway because of the strays — and the role-suffix words themselves still carry it.

content.text:
No — nothing stays uppercase. That paragraph was me naming the *cost* of the plan, not an exception to it: the one thing the all-lowercase rule sacrifices is the visual pop where uppercase files stood out from the `_cli.sh`/`_regime.sh` boilerplate in a directory listing. I was acknowledging the loss and arguing it's acceptable, since the role words (`cli`, `regime`, `workbench`) still tell you which files are boilerplate even without a case signal. Every `.sh` file ends up lowercase under the proposal.

And yes, BUK was considered in full — it's just that BUK is already almost entirely compliant. Its 39 shell files are all lowercase snake_case except one stray, `bupr_PresentationRegime.sh` → `bupr_presentation_regime.sh` (or a shortened single word, per the same slimming pass). Same for jjk, cmk, vvk, vok, and apck: zero renames needed, they're already clean. The entire rename population is rbk's 41 files plus that one BUK stray, plus any PascalCase test-case files under the `rbtc*` pattern.

So the full shape of the plan:

1. **One rule**: `«prefix»_«snake_name».sh`, prefer single word — no carve-outs for decomposition clusters or test cases.
2. **Rename** the 42 files (plus test-case files), shortening multi-word names to a single word where the prefix already carries the meaning (`rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` is the poster child).
3. **Sweep references**: sourcing lines, zipper registry, rbk acronym map, CLAUDE.md mint table row, BCG's three PascalCase table rows.

If you want to slate this as a pace, the word-shortening choices (which multi-word names slim to what) are the only judgment calls in it — the rest is mechanical.

message.content: This is an excellent pace for Bb; please slate it and leave it detailed I think, there's nothing too sensitive.  Though an early step might be to make sure the docket map is correct.  Firming up the wording of BCG ought to be part of it.  Slate the pace, or raise more issues first.

input.content:
# jjezs_slate bash-filename-case-consolidation

Consolidate every bash implementation filename to one rule:
`«prefix»_«snake_name».sh`, all lowercase, prefer a single word —
retiring BCG's PascalCase carve-outs so no mixed-case `.sh` file remains under `Tools/`.

## Character
Intricate but mechanical;
the only judgment calls are the word-shortening choices on multi-word names.

## Cinched
- One rule, no carve-outs:
  plain modules, decomposition entries (`«prefix»0_`), decomposition clusters, and test-case files all go lowercase.
- Where a PascalCase name is multi-word and the prefix already carries the meaning,
  slim the word part to a single word rather than transliterating to long snake_case
  (the foundry-director-build module is the poster child).
- Doc files (`ACRONYM-Words.ext`) and tabtarget frontispieces are separate naming grammars — untouched.
- The distributed kits (buk/jjk/cmk/vvk/vok/apck) are already compliant except a single buk stray;
  the rename population is essentially rbk-internal, which is why lowercase won over uppercase as the consolidation direction.

## Done when
- A case-sensitive sweep for uppercase letters in `.sh` basenames under `Tools/` lands empty.
- A repo-wide grep for each retired basename lands clean —
  sourcing lines, zipper registry, specs, acronym maps, launcher configs.
- BCG's Naming Convention Patterns table states the single lowercase rule plainly,
  with the PascalCase rows (decomposition entry, decomposition cluster, test-case file) recut to match.
- The CLAUDE.md mint table's code-file row (`prefix_Word.ext`) and the rbk acronym map agree with the new names.
- The `fast` test suite passes; run a broader tier if touched files warrant it.

First step at mount: rebuild the rename map fresh —
enumerate offenders with a case-sensitive sweep (e.g. `find Tools -name '*.sh' | grep '[A-Z]'`)
rather than trusting any slate-time list, then confirm the map with the operator before renaming.
Case-only renames on macOS's case-insensitive filesystem want `git mv`.
Single-operator caution: land renames and reference updates in the same commit so the tree never sources a missing file.

content.text:
Slated: **₢BbAAL** (bash-filename-case-consolidation) on ₣Bb, at the end of the heat.

The docket carries the full shape as discussed:

- **Cinched**: one lowercase rule with no carve-outs; multi-word names slim to a single word where the prefix carries the meaning; doc files and tabtarget frontispieces untouched; rationale note that the rename population is rbk-internal (why lowercase won).
- **Done when**: case-sweep of `Tools/` lands empty, retired basenames grep clean repo-wide, BCG's table recut to state the single rule plainly, CLAUDE.md mint row and rbk acronym map agree, fast suite passes.
- **Your early-step concern** is the docket's first instruction at mount: rebuild the rename map fresh with a case-sensitive sweep rather than trusting any slate-time list, and confirm the map with you before renaming.

I also added two mechanical cautions: `git mv` for case-only renames on macOS, and landing each rename with its reference updates in the same commit so the tree never sources a missing file.

No further issues to raise — the one thing I'd flag for mount-time rather than now is that the word-shortening choices (e.g. what `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild` slims to) will want your eye, and the docket routes that through the map-confirmation step.

content: We agreed to consolidate all bash filenames to lowercase snake_case and slated it as pace ₢BbAAL on heat ₣Bb. Next action: mount that pace when ready; it starts by rebuilding the rename map for your confirmation.
